questions.question_text: Which agency administers ATCP 134, Wisconsin's residential rental practices rule?
questions.correct_answer: Department of Agriculture, Trade and Consumer Protection
distractors: Department of Safety and Professional Services
questions.explanation: ATCP 134 is administered by the Department of Agriculture, Trade and Consumer Protection (DATCP). It governs residential rental agreements, disclosures, security deposits, and prohibited landlord practices.

questions.question_text: Under ATCP 134, a landlord fails to disclose known building code violations to a new tenant. This is:
questions.correct_answer: A violation of the landlord's disclosure obligations
distractors: Acceptable if the violations are minor
Only a problem if the tenant discovers them
Permitted if the property passes inspection
questions.explanation: ATCP 134 requires landlords to disclose building code violations, structural defects, and other material conditions. Failing to disclose known violations is a clear violation of the rule.

questions.question_text: A tenant reports a mold problem, and the landlord raises the rent the next month. Under Wisconsin law, this may be:
distractors: Legal if the rent increase is under 10%
Only illegal if the tenant has a written lease
questions.explanation: Wisconsin law prohibits landlords from retaliating against tenants who exercise their legal rights, such as reporting habitability issues. A rent increase shortly after a complaint may constitute illegal retaliation.

questions.question_text: What duty does a Wisconsin landlord owe regarding the physical condition of rental property?
distractors: Only repair items listed in the lease
Provide repairs only if tenant pays for them
Maintain only common areas, not individual units
questions.explanation: Under both ATCP 134 and Chapter 704, landlords must maintain rental premises in habitable condition. This includes functioning plumbing, heating, electrical, and structural integrity.

questions.question_text: A tenant pays rent in cash. What must the landlord provide?
questions.correct_answer: A receipt for the cash payment
distractors: Nothing, cash payments need no documentation
A letter to the tenant's bank confirming payment
questions.explanation: ATCP 134 requires landlords to provide receipts for cash payments. This protects both parties by creating documentation of rent payment.

questions.question_text: Does Wisconsin have a statutory maximum for security deposits?
questions.correct_answer: No, there is no statutory maximum
questions.explanation: Unlike many states, Wisconsin has no statutory cap on security deposit amounts. However, excessive deposits may be challenged as unreasonable under general contract principles.

questions.question_text: A tenant moves out on March 15. By when must the landlord return the security deposit?
questions.explanation: Wisconsin requires landlords to return the security deposit within 21 days of lease termination. The landlord must provide an itemized statement of any deductions.

questions.question_text: A landlord deducts cleaning costs from a security deposit. What must accompany the remaining balance?
distractors: Receipts from the cleaning company only
questions.explanation: When withholding any portion of the security deposit, the landlord must provide an itemized statement of deductions showing what was withheld and why, along with the remaining balance.

questions.question_text: A new tenant moves in. How many days do they have to complete the check-in inspection sheet?
questions.explanation: When a security deposit is required, tenants have 7 days from the start of tenancy to inspect the premises and return a completed check-in sheet noting any existing defects. This protects the tenant at move-out.

questions.question_text: A lease provision states the tenant waives their right to security deposit return. Is this enforceable?
questions.correct_answer: No, this is a prohibited provision under ATCP 134
distractors: Yes, if the tenant signed the lease voluntarily
Only if the deposit is under $500
questions.explanation: ATCP 134 prohibits lease provisions that waive a tenant's right to security deposit return. Such clauses are void and unenforceable, regardless of whether the tenant agreed to them.

questions.question_text: A month-to-month tenancy in Wisconsin is an example of which type of lease?
questions.explanation: A month-to-month lease is a periodic tenancy — it automatically renews at the end of each period unless either party gives proper notice. Year-to-year leases are also periodic tenancies.

questions.question_text: A tenant has a 2-year lease that ends on a specific date. This is classified as:
questions.explanation: A tenancy for years has a fixed term with a definite end date. Despite the name, it can be for any specific duration — it does not need to be measured in years.

questions.question_text: Under Chapter 704, what distinguishes a tenancy at will from other lease types?
questions.correct_answer: No fixed term; either party may terminate at any time
distractors: The tenant has no obligation to pay rent
Only the landlord can terminate the tenancy
It automatically converts to a year-to-year lease
questions.explanation: A tenancy at will has no fixed term and can be terminated by either party at any time with proper notice. It's the most flexible but least secure tenancy type.

questions.question_text: A lease clause requires the tenant to pay the landlord's attorney fees regardless of who wins a dispute. Is this enforceable?
questions.correct_answer: No, this is a prohibited provision under ATCP 134
distractors: Yes, if both parties agreed to the clause
Yes, for leases over 1 year in duration
Only if the attorney fees are under $1,000
questions.explanation: ATCP 134 prohibits lease provisions requiring tenants to pay landlord's attorney fees regardless of outcome. It also prohibits clauses authorizing property confiscation and clauses waiving the landlord's duty to mitigate damages.

questions.question_text: Which Wisconsin statute primarily governs landlord-tenant relationships?
questions.explanation: Chapter 704 governs landlord-tenant relationships generally, defining tenancy types and notice requirements. ATCP 134 provides additional protections. Chapter 452 covers real estate licensing, 709 covers condition reports, and 766 covers marital property.
